description: This schematic diagram illustrates the selective laser melting (SLM) preparation process of Fe-30Mn-9Al-C-3Ni low-density steel. The main panel depicts the SLM apparatus configuration, featuring a laser beam interacting with Fe30Mn9AlC3Ni0.6Cr powder on a substrate, accompanied by a powder feed container and overflow container. The magnified inset on the lower right details the melt pool formation mechanism, showing the laser beam moving direction, the melt pool geometry, and the layer-by-layer building process on the substrate. The lower left inset presents the scanning strategy, specifically illustrating a 67° rotation angle between adjacent layers (Layer n and Layer n+1) to minimize laser vector coincidence and reduce crystallographic texture. Caption and citation context indicate the use of an SLM Solutions SLM280 printer with substrate preheating to 200°C, argon atmosphere protection (oxygen <300 ppm), laser powers ranging from 70 to 150 W (energy densities 58.33-125.00 J/mm³), scanning speed of 750 mm/s, spot diameter of 0.1 mm, layer thickness of 0.02 mm, and hatch space of 0.08 mm. This figure establishes the experimental setup and processing parameters that enable the fabrication of lightweight Fe-Mn-Al-C-Ni alloys with controlled microstructure and mechanical properties.
